claim_boundary: Ticket 37 searches for pointwise rank candidates and bounded counterexamples to weak ranks. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Transfer Ticket 37's lesson: a bounded rank candidate becomes useful only if it names the pointwise extension theorem. Aggregate evidence and density controls remain support, not proof.

bounded_result.bounded_rank_analogue: A positive kernel/rank must rule out each off-critical zero, not only a sampled or density-small family.
bounded_result.discarded_route: finite-height zero verification or smoothed average positivity treated as RH proof
bounded_result.retained_route: pointwise positivity certificate strong enough to contradict every off-critical zero

attempts.obstruction: The remaining obstruction is pointwise. Sparse exceptional objects can survive every bounded or averaged test unless a rank or positivity theorem excludes each one.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every off-critical zero forces a negative certificate in an explicitly positive kernel, so no off-critical zero can exist.
attempts.next_experiment: Build adversarial off-critical zero configurations and test every proposed kernel for pointwise failure.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Do not discard Ticket 36's natural frontier audit. Extend it into a rank-synthesis test: small linear rank candidates are attacked by bounded counterexamples, while the surviving piecewise candidate is kept only as a theorem target.

candidate_piecewise_linear_rank.candidate: n >= 128 implies exit_bits <= 11 * bit_length(n); all tested n imply exit_bits <= 12 * bit_length(n)
candidate_piecewise_linear_rank.reason_not_proof: The scan gives a sharp bounded candidate, but it does not prove that future residue classes cannot produce larger ratios. A proof needs a symbolic extension lemma over all adaptive frontier states.

pointwise_rank_probe.closed_bounded_statement: Every tested odd n <= 5000000 exits the adaptive null frontier by 320 probe bits. In the same sample, exit_bits <= 12 * bit_length(n), and for n >= 128, exit_bits <= 11 * bit_length(n).
pointwise_rank_probe.proof_boundary: This is not a Collatz proof. It only identifies a non-circular-looking bounded rank shape. The missing step is a symbolic theorem proving that all future natural residues obey the same piecewise linear rank.

discard: exit_bits <= 8 * bit_length(n) as a global rank
exit_bits <= 9 * bit_length(n) as a global rank
exit_bits <= 10 * bit_length(n) as a global rank
unqualified exit_bits <= 11 * bit_length(n) without finite seed handling
retain: finite seed set plus exit_bits <= 11 * bit_length(n) for n >= 128 as a bounded theorem candidate
global exit_bits <= 12 * bit_length(n) as a weaker bounded theorem candidate
symbolic extension lemma over adaptive frontier states

attempts.obstruction: The experiment found no bounded counterexample to the 12x rank shape, but 8x, 9x, 10x, and unqualified 11x ranks are refuted in the bounded sample. The surviving rank is empirical and still lacks an infinite extension theorem.
attempts.candidate_theorem: There is a finite verified seed set S such that every positive odd n not in S exits the adaptive null frontier by 11 * bit_length(n), and each seed in S is independently resolved.
attempts.next_experiment: Search for a symbolic frontier extension lemma: every open residue block either exits within O(delta bits) or maps to a smaller verified seed without invoking Collatz stopping time.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified Collatz counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Transfer Ticket 37's lesson: a bounded rank candidate becomes useful only if it names the pointwise extension theorem. Aggregate evidence and density controls remain support, not proof.

bounded_result.bounded_rank_analogue: A cutoff rank must give a positive representation lower bound for each even N beyond N0.
bounded_result.discarded_route: density-positive or almost-all Goldbach theorem treated as pointwise Goldbach proof
bounded_result.retained_route: explicit lower bound with a finite verified seed interval

attempts.obstruction: The remaining obstruction is pointwise. Sparse exceptional objects can survive every bounded or averaged test unless a rank or positivity theorem excludes each one.
attempts.candidate_theorem: There is an explicit N0 such that every even N>N0 has a positive binary-prime representation count, with all N<=N0 verified.
attempts.next_experiment: Search for sparse even-number adversaries that pass density checks but minimize pointwise representation counts.
attempts.claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Transfer Ticket 37's lesson: a bounded rank candidate becomes useful only if it names the pointwise extension theorem. Aggregate evidence and density controls remain support, not proof.

bounded_result.bounded_rank_analogue: A gap selector must force exact gap 2 for arbitrarily large ranges rather than leak mass into other bounded gaps.
bounded_result.discarded_route: bounded-gap or averaged pair mass treated as twin-prime infinitude
bounded_result.retained_route: exact gap-2 lower bound with leakage control against all wider admissible gaps

attempts.obstruction: The remaining obstruction is pointwise. Sparse exceptional objects can survive every bounded or averaged test unless a rank or positivity theorem excludes each one.
attempts.candidate_theorem: For arbitrarily large X, an unconditional nonnegative weight leaves positive mass on exact gap 2 after all wider-gap leakage is subtracted.
attempts.next_experiment: Stress candidate sieve weights against models that preserve bounded gaps while deleting exact gap-2 pairs.
attempts.claim_boundary: No twin-prime proof and no certified twin-prime counterexample.
